'''Bill Leyland''' (born 3 February 2003) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|hk}} for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] in the [[Super League]], on a short-term loan from [[Hull Kingston Rovers]].

==Background==
Leyland was born in [[Maidstone, Kent]], England. His brother [[Oliver Leyland|Oli Leyland]] is a fellow [[London Broncos|London]] player.<ref>[https://www.standard.co.uk/news/london/brothers-oli-bill-inspire-pupils-rugby-league-london-broncos-b1009633.html Brothers inspire next generation to try rugby league at London Broncos]</ref>

He played for the Invicta Panthers as a junior.<ref>[https://www.zeeon.co.uk/away-player/bill-leyland Bill Leyland]</ref>

Leyland attended [[Maidstone Grammar School]]<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://londonbroncosrl.com/2022/06/28/leyland-brothers-inspire-the-next-generation-to-try-out-rugby-league/ |title=Leyland brothers inspire the next generation to try out rugby league |access-date=2023-10-16 |archive-date=2023-10-18 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231018203812/https://londonbroncosrl.com/2022/06/28/leyland-brothers-inspire-the-next-generation-to-try-out-rugby-league/ |url-status=dead }}</ref> and graduated from [[St Mary's University, Twickenham|St Marys University]] in [[Twickenham, London]].<ref>[https://www.stmarys.ac.uk/sport/scholars/bill-leyland Bill Leyland - Sport: Rugby League]</ref>

==Playing career==
===London Broncos===
He joined the [[London Broncos]] first team squad at the start of the [[2022 RFL Championship|2022 season]].

He made his professional debut for the London Broncos in 2022 against the [[York Knights]].<ref>[https://londonbroncosrl.com/2023/07/13/bill-leyland-re-signs-for-broncos/ Bill Leyland Re-signs for Broncos]</ref>

Leyland was the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] young player of the year in 2023.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/62224/-rugby-league-awards-night-2023-%E2%80%93-the-winners|title=Rugby League Awards Night 2023 – the winners|website=www.rugby-league.com}}</ref>

On 15 October 2023, Leyland played in the [[2023 RFL Championship#Week 3: Grand Final|Championship Grand Final]] victory against [[Toulouse Olympique]] in France.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/we-said-we-were-going-to-do-it-mike-eccles-revels-in-london-broncos-super-league-return-4372839|title='We said we were going to do it': Mike Eccles revels in London Broncos' Super League return}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/67105947|title=Championship Grand Final: Toulouse 14-18 London Broncos - Capital club back in Super League|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>

===Hull Kingston Rovers===
On 15 July 2024 it was reported that he had signed for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the [[Super League]] on a 2-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=BBC Sport|date=15 Jul 2024|accessdate=3 Oct 2024|title=Hull KR sign Broncos hooker Leyland|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cgrlg22845wo}}</ref>

===Huddersfield Giants (loan)===
On 9 April 2025 it was reported that he had signed for the [[Huddersfield Giants]] in the [[Super League]] on loan.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=9 Apr 2025|author=Ben Olawumi|title=Huddersfield Giants snap up Hull KR hooker as highly-rated starlet tied down|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/huddersfield-giants-snap-up-hull-kr-hooker-as-highly-rated-starlet-tied-down}}</ref>

===St Helens (loan)===
On 31 March 2026, Leyland signed for fellow Super League club [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] on a short term loan alongside [[Jordan Dezaria]]. Leyland made his debut on 3 April as a substitute in the 'Rivals Round' [[St Helens R.F.C.–Wigan Warriors rivalry|derby]] against the [[Wigan Warriors]] at the [[BrewDog Stadium]], scoring two late tries to secure a 34-24 comeback victory.<ref>{{cite news |last1=Freeman |first1=Jay |title=Most impactful bow ever? Loanee Leyland's stunning Saints show |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c5y80129p91o |access-date=4 April 2026 |work=BBC Sport |date=3 April 2026}}</ref>

On 28 April 2026 it was reported that he had returned to [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] in the [[Super League]] on a second one-week loan<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=28 Apr 2026|author=Matthew Shaw|title=St Helens secure return of hero Bill Leyland in huge boost|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/st-helens-secure-return-of-hero-bill-leyland-in-huge-boost}}</ref>
